name: Apple-Sour Cream Streusel Tart
recipeIngredient: 6 Tablespoons Cold Butter or Margarine, Cut into Small Pieces
1/3 Cup Cold Stick Butter or Margarine (Not Spread), Cut into Small Pieces
1 1/4 Pounds (3 Medium) Granny Smith Apples, Peeled, Quartered, Cored and Cut into Thin Slices
recipeInstructions:
1. To make dough in food processor: Pulse flour, sugar, salt, and butter until coarse crumbs form. Sprinkle water over the crumbs; pulse just until the mixture clumps together. Remove the blade, then shape the dough.
2. By hand: Mix flour, sugar, and salt in a medium bowl. Cut in butter with a pastry blender until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Sprinkle with water and stir with a fork until the mixture forms a dough. Shape into a 1/2-inch-thick disk.
3. Place the dough into a 9-in. tart pan with removable bottom. Firmly press it over the bottom and up the sides of the pan. Place in the freezer. Preheat oven to 375 degrees F.
4. Press a sheet of foil onto the dough. Bake for 15 minutes or until the crust is dry. Remove the foil; bake the crust for an additional 10 minutes or until starting to brown around the top edge. Cool on a wire rack.
5. Meanwhile, mix Streusel ingredients in a medium bowl and rub together with fingers until crumbly. Refrigerate.
6. For the filling: Whisk sour cream, sugar, egg, flour, and extract in a large bowl until blended. Add apple slices and toss to mix. Spread into a pie plate; sprinkle streusel evenly over top (yes, there's a lot).
7. Bake for 1 hour or until the streusel is lightly browned and apples are tender when pierced. If nuts in the streusel brown too quickly, loosely drape the reserved foil over the pie.
8. Remove to a wire rack. Cool completely before removing pan sides. Serve at room temperature or refrigerate up to 2 days.
